results.title: WizeMe.APP: Private AI Continuity and Decision OS
results.content:
Private continuity for people carrying too much context

# Stop losing the thread. WizeMe remembers what matters so you move.

One private partner turns scattered apps, meetings, notes, relationships, and open loops into clearer decisions and follow-through.

Scattered  meetings decisions people routines  become remembered next moves.

Start free with one thread Read common questions See plans

Trusted by privacy-minded operators 146 reviews across rendered routes 0 private memories exposed 0 blocking route issues Launch pricing available Memory-aware decisions

Start here

## Bring one real thing.

A decision, meeting, relationship, habit, or open loop. WizeMe turns it into a cleaner next move before asking you to learn the product. [...] ### 2. Build continuity

Your partner can remember decisions, constraints, corrections, people, and outcomes with permission, then use that context to make the next conversation sharper.

### 3. Follow through

Morning briefs, nightly debriefs, hard-talk prep, meeting prep, Board of 10, reminders, and routines turn insight into action instead of another open loop.

## Built for people whose context is already too valuable to keep scattered.

Founders, operators, professionals, creators, students, caregivers, and ambitious people can use WizeMe to connect work, family, health, goals, notes, relationships, and decisions. It is not a replacement for human judgment. It is a continuity layer that helps you notice what matters sooner and act with less re-explaining. [...] ## Stop making every app, meeting, note, and open loop compete for your attention.

Most productivity tools store fragments. WizeMe is designed to turn those fragments into remembered decisions, next moves, relationship context, routines, and useful briefs. The point is not another dashboard. The point is a private partner that helps you carry context forward without making your work or life feel managed by a machine.

## How WizeMe helps

### 1. Bring one real thing

Start with a decision, goal, person, meeting, habit, health signal, note, or pressure point. WizeMe uses a guided first session when you are new and a direct app path when you are returning.

### 2. Build continuity

results.title: Opal: Private Memory for Personal AI
results.content:
Unlike client-local memory, where the device must store and search all personal
data itself (win, (#bib.bib14)), Opal keeps minimal state on the client (a
secret key kk and a monotonic counter 𝖼𝗍𝗋\mathsf{ctr}; see
§3.4): the
logical client only submits pre-chunked fixed-size items to 𝖤𝗇𝖼𝗅𝖺𝗏𝖾𝖮𝗉𝖺𝗅\mathsf{Enclave}\_{\mathsf{Opal}}, which
handles storage and retrieval on ORAM-backed disk. An alternative deployment
could place a normalized ingestion pipeline at the application provider, as is
common in production systems (Chen et al., 2016; Baylor et al., 2017; Bittau et al., 2017).222This would require a different freshness mechanism (e.g., a
verifiable randomness beacon (Kelsey et al., 2019)) in place of the monotonic
counter in §3.4. A single enclave serves multiple [...] ##### Report GitHub Issue

Content selection saved. Describe the issue below:

arXiv logo

# Opal: Private Memory for Personal AI

###### Abstract.

Personal AI systems increasingly retain long-term memory of user activity,
including documents, emails, messages, meetings, and ambient recordings. Trusted
hardware can keep this data private, but struggles to scale with a growing
datastore. This pushes the data to external storage, which exposes retrieval
access patterns that leak private information to the application provider.
Oblivious RAM (ORAM) is a cryptographic primitive that can hide these patterns,
but it requires a fixed access budget, precluding the query-dependent traversals
that agentic memory systems rely on for accuracy. [...] YlgVN03a9Xu8jgCNCyIegIAgx13Vfd7vdu+FweG8YRkjXdWy329+dTgeSJD3ieZ7RNO0VAXAPwDEAO5VKndi2fWrb9jWl9Esul6PZbDY9Go1OZ7PZ9z/lyuD3OozU2wAAAABJRU5ErkJggg==)

# 11 Best AI Second Me Alternatives to Build Your Personal AI (2025 Guide)

Updated at Sep 18, 2025

9 min

# 11 Best AI Second Me Alternatives to Build Your Personal AI (2025 Guide)

If you've been exploring AI Second Me for creating a personalized AI that mirrors your style, routines, and knowledge, you're not alone. The “personal AI” category is booming—spanning memory-centric agents, autonomous workflows, and privacy-first assistants. In this guide, we’ll unpack what AI Second Me is, why people are seeking alternatives, and the top tools worth considering in 2025. [...] ### 8) Memory-First Personal AI Platforms (Category)

 Why it matters: Your “AI Second Me” needs continuity—memory of your past tasks, your documents, and your tone. The second-brain category (e.g., open-source note + RAG stacks) provides the memory layer many personal AIs need.

 How to build: Pair an open-source note system (Obsidian/Markdown repos) with vector databases and a local LLM via Open WebUI—then wrap with agents that remember user-specific guidance.

### 9) Agentic Email + Calendar Specialists (Category)

 Why it’s relevant: Many users define “my AI” as something that handles email, scheduling, and follow-ups in their voice. Look for tools with strong identity modeling and guardrails for safe sending.
